articles.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets

Imperva buried instructions inside shared contacts, vCards, and location pins that the agent executed without the victim ever seeing them. Varonis built a test agent on

analysis.desc: Hidden instructions in vCards and location pins bypass visible security checks, exploiting agent's automatic processing.

articles.title: AI Broke Vulnerability Management. That's Why CISOs Are Moving Budget to BAS.

articles.title: Microsoft patches Exchange Server zero-day exploited in attacks
articles.summary: Microsoft has patched an actively exploited Exchange Server vulnerability that allows threat actors to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks targeting Outlook Web Access users. [...]

keypoints: Exchange Server zero-day actively exploited in wild
XSS vulnerability targets Outlook Web Access users

analysis.title: Attack Vector and Impact
analysis.desc: Threat actors exploit XSS flaw in Exchange Server to inject malicious JavaScript code targeting OWA users, enabling session hijacking and credential theft.

analysis.title: Affected Infrastructure
analysis.desc: Exchange Server deployments exposed. OWA interfaces vulnerable. Organizations running unpatched versions face immediate risk from active exploitation campaigns.

analysis.title: Mitigation Priority
analysis.desc: Microsoft patch released and available. Immediate deployment critical. Organizations should prioritize patching Exchange Server instances before attackers expand campaign scope.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Exchange Server Zero-Day Under Active Attack — Patch Now
ai_summary.bottom_line: Attackers are actively exploiting your Exchange Server right now — patch within hours, not days.
ai_summary.action_today: Exchange Admin → Check Version → Download Latest Patch → Deploy Immediately

articles.title: Microsoft: Some Windows PCs fail to install latest monthly updates
articles.summary: Microsoft warned customers on Tuesday that they may have issues installing the latest monthly updates on some Windows devices that were upgraded to Windows 11 24H2 or 25H2. [...]

keypoints: Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2 update installation failures
Microsoft acknowledges widespread monthly patch deployment issues

analysis.title: Update Deployment Breakdown
analysis.desc: Windows devices running latest versions experience installation failures preventing critical security patches from deploying successfully.

analysis.title: Affected User Base
analysis.desc: Systems upgraded to Windows 11 24H2 or 25H2 face highest risk of blocked updates leaving them vulnerable.

analysis.title: Microsoft Response Status
analysis.desc: Company acknowledged issue publicly but resolution timeline unclear, leaving customers without immediate remediation path forward.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Windows 11 Update Failures Block Critical Security Patches
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your Windows 11 PC may be stuck without security fixes while attackers exploit known vulnerabilities.
ai_summary.action_today: Settings → System → Windows Update → Check for updates (manual retry)

articles.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
articles.summary: Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem. Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report looks stable. Leadership reads "stable" as "secure." It usually isn't. The work slows down. The risk does not. That gap is what a The Hacker News webinar with Picus Security sets out to close. Autumn

keypoints: Automated pentests plateau, missing critical vulnerabilities
False security from stable reports hides real risk

analysis.title: Automation's diminishing returns
analysis.desc: Repeated automated scans find fewer issues over time, creating false confidence in security posture despite unchanged actual risk.

analysis.title: Gap between perception and reality
analysis.desc: Leadership interprets stable reports as secure systems, missing that risk continues while detection slows or plateaus entirely.

analysis.title: Expert-guided assessment needed
analysis.desc: Webinar addresses how human-driven penetration testing uncovers vulnerabilities automation consistently misses in mature environments.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Penetration Testing: Why Automated Scans Miss Critical Vulnerabilities
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your clean pentest report might be masking serious exposures your tools can't see.
ai_summary.action_today: Schedule manual penetration testing review beyond automated scanning tools with security team this week

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
articles.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release. Of the 206 flaws, 39 are rated Critical, and 167 are rated Important in severity. This includes 63 privilege escalation, 56 remote code execution, 30 information disclosure, 27 spoofing, 20 security

keypoints: Microsoft patches record 206 vulnerabilities including three zero-days
39 critical flaws with 56 remote code execution bugs discovered

analysis.title: Scale of vulnerability outbreak
analysis.desc: 206 flaws across Microsoft portfolio represents unprecedented patch volume. Critical severity dominates with 39 rated Critical and 167 Important.

analysis.title: Exploitation risk categories
analysis.desc: 56 remote code execution bugs enable direct system takeover. 63 privilege escalation flaws allow attackers to gain admin access.

analysis.title: Zero-day disclosure timing
analysis.desc: Three zero-day flaws publicly disclosed at patch release. Active exploitation risk immediate without rapid deployment of available fixes.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Microsoft Emergency: 206 Vulnerabilities Including Live Zero-Days
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your Windows machine is actively targetable right now — patch within 24 hours or disconnect it.
ai_summary.action_today: Windows → Settings → Update & Security → Windows Update → Check for updates → Install now
